context: The first thing you do in any shop, bar or street in Spain is greet someone. A warm "hola" and a "gracias" instantly change how locals treat you - even if you say nothing else in Spanish. This is the single highest-value thing to learn first.
explanation: Start with the three you'll use constantly: "hola" (hello) and "adios" (goodbye) open and close almost any interaction, at any time of day, and "gracias" is the politeness word you'll reach for most.

examples.note: Opening and closing, any time of day.
A friendly way to enter a shop.
The polite way to accept or decline.

phrases.note: Works at any time of day.
